summary: postinstall exfiltrates CTF flags and host identifiers to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a Linux-only payload guarded by sandbox-evasion checks: it returns early when the cwd is under /tmp/npm-safe or /tmp/pkg, or when the hostname contains nijin or hetzner. When those checks pass, the script reads /app/src/main.rs, /app/flag.txt, /flag, /flag.txt, runs `grep -RaoE 'HTB\{[^}]+\}' /app` to harvest CTF flag strings, collects hostname, uid, cwd, and a file listing, and issues an HTTP PUT carrying the JSON body to http://154.57.164.82:31250/api/modules/ECT-987654 (with 127.0.0.1 fallbacks). The combination of bare-IP plaintext exfiltration endpoint, environment-gated execution to evade analysis, and targeted reads of installer-side secret files is unambiguous attacker infrastructure designed to steal data from CI/build hosts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
